title: antirrhinum repens var. monspessulanum
intro: Antirrhinum repens var. monspessulanum, also known as the snapdragon of the Pyrenees, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Plantaginaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, cuts, and skin irritations. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of Antirrhinum repens var. monspessulanum are used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
